degraded.detail: shared/scripts/tools/check-external-review-keys.py (the documented pre-flight key-check wrapper) does not exist — the tooling gap SS6 will fix. external_review.py itself IS functional: --mode code ran GPT-5.4 + Gemini 3.1 Pro via OpenRouter successfully on this diff (OpenAI: ship-with-fixes, 4 findings, all addressed; Gemini: no findings). External review was therefore NOT degraded for this run; only the missing wrapper is.

F0 ran shipwright-run (282) + shipwright-plan (57) + integration-tests/test_single_session_pipeline.py (3) + shared/tests (3739 passed, 1 pre-fix canon lint now green) + ruff@0.15.15 + leak-guard f0 ALLOW. The full all-plugin suite runs in CI on the PR.

surface_verification.justification: SS4 is a Python plugin change: a phase-runner agent def, the single_session on-disk persistence guard + reload module + a new single-session-reload CLI subcommand, the section-writer write-path fix + a non-blocking SubagentStop fallback hook, and docs. No web/UI/API surface, no server, no client code. run_config is only READ by the reload/guard (all mutation stays in phase_task_lifecycle); no message-contract change, so the Backend-affects-Frontend rule does not apply. The CLI surface was exercised END-TO-END: integration-tests/test_single_session_pipeline.py drives single-session-next / single-session-apply through a full pipeline INCLUDING the new persistence guard, plus a cross_component scenario proving a claimed-but-unwritten artifact is rejected fail-closed across the real subprocess CLI. Verification = F0 pytest (339 unit + 3 integration + 3739 shared) + ruff + artifact-path-canon + leak-guard f0 ALLOW.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 — phase-runner agent has a write path (Write/Edit/Bash)
AC1 — phase-runner brief mandates disk persistence, not a Stop hook
AC1/AC4 — a claimed-but-unwritten artifact is caught by the on-disk guard
AC4 — an ok result whose artifacts exist on disk completes; ok=False skips the guard
AC4 — a stale apply reports stale_version, not artifacts_missing (CAS precedence)
AC3 — reload rebuilds context from compact summaries, transcript-blind + budget-bounded
AC3 — reload is read-only and returns None on no/corrupt/non-v2 config
AC3 — the summary ceiling (context-budget guard) is enforced at write-time by the contract
AC3 — single-session-reload CLI subcommand + cli.main routing wired at runtime
AC2 — section-writer declares a Write path + a direct-write instruction
AC2 — hook is a no-op when the section file already exists (no clobber, no block)
AC2 — hook salvages from the transcript only when the file is missing (env dir set)
AC2 — salvage write refuses an untrusted inferred dir outside the project tree
AC2 — salvage refuses content that is not a section document
AC2 — the hook NEVER blocks (bad payload / missing transcript_path / empty / unsalvageable)
cross_component — hook + loop + result-contract compose end-to-end via the real CLI
behaviors.evidence: integration-tests/test_single_session_pipeline.py::TestPersistenceGuardCrossComponent::test_claimed_but_unwritten_artifact_is_rejected PASSED (subprocess CLI, artifacts_missing, run stays in_progress, frontier intact)

behaviors.behavior: the live LLM phase-runner/section-writer actually writes its files at runtime
behaviors.reason: A real subagent's file writes cannot be exercised in a unit test; the behavior is enforced fail-closed by the on-disk artifact guard (single-session) and check-sections.py Step 7 (plan), both of which ARE tested.
